title: linum hirsutum subsp. byzantinum
intro: Linum hirsutum subsp. byzantinum, also known as flax, is a subspecies of the flax plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. The health benefits of Linum hirsutum subsp. byzantinum include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting skin health. Herbal preparations of flax include oil, seeds, and infusions, which can be used to support various health conditions, including reducing menstrual cramps and improving the symptoms of menopause.
